title: League: Four Factors
notes: Exact public Cleaning the Glass stats guide for efficiency and Dean Oliver's four factors. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: The four factors explain why teams score efficiently: effective field goal percentage, turnover rate, offensive rebounding rate, and free throw rate. Offensive rebounding rate differs from raw offensive rebounds because it measures how often a team grabs available missed shots, normalizing second-chance creation for pace, missed-shot opportunities, and team style.

title: Basketball Reference Glossary
notes: Stable glossary source for advanced stat definitions. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary; do not commit copied glossary text.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: VORP means Value Over Replacement Player. VORP is calculated from Box Plus/Minus style box-score impact, replacement level, possessions, and playing time to estimate value above a replacement player. Box Plus/Minus, or BPM, estimates a player's contribution per 100 possessions from box-score inputs and has limitations because it cannot directly see defensive positioning, screen assists, spacing, or on/off context. Usage rate estimates how often a player finishes or directly uses team possessions through shots, free throws, assists in some models, or turnovers, so it gives context when comparing efficiency at different offensive loads. True shooting percentage improves on field goal percentage by accounting for threes and free throws.

title: Shooting Efficiency Metrics
notes: Exact public Cleaning the Glass shooting frequency and accuracy guide. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Analysts prefer true shooting percentage over field goal percentage because true shooting includes the extra value of three-pointers and the scoring value of free throws. Field goal percentage treats every make the same and ignores free throws, while true shooting better reflects total scoring efficiency from shots and trips to the line.

title: Play Type Analysis
notes: Synergy source was not fetchable during live pass; includes original repo-authored play-type summary and avoids restricted or paywalled material.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Play type analysis groups possessions by the action that creates the shot or turnover. Common NBA play types include pick-and-roll ball handler, pick-and-roll roll man, isolation, post-up, spot-up, transition, off-screen, handoff, cut, and putback. Analysts use play types to see which actions an offense relies on and how efficiently each action generates points.

title: Positive Possession Rate
notes: Exact public Cleaning the Glass play-context guide for possession context and play outcomes. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Positive possession rate is a way to describe how often half-court possessions end in useful outcomes. Positive possessions usually include made shots, free throw attempts, or other outcomes that create efficient scoring value, while empty possessions include missed shots without an offensive rebound, turnovers, or late-clock failures.

title: Zone Defense Usage Is Up, But Teams Have Solutions
notes: NBA.com robots check timed out during live pass; includes original repo-authored zone-defense summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Zone defense assigns defenders to areas instead of one-on-one matchups. NBA teams use zone to change rhythm, protect weak individual defenders, shrink driving lanes, and hide matchup problems. The tradeoff is that zones can give up open three-point shots, offensive rebounds, and quick ball-movement advantages if rotations are late.

title: Nikola Jokic Offensive Profile
notes: Exact public Thinking Basketball article on Jokic's postseason offensive profile. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Nikola Jokic is often described as a historic offensive center because he combines elite true shooting efficiency, post scoring, passing, and primary playmaking. His offense is unusual because a center can initiate from the elbows, post, short roll, and top of the floor while creating high-quality shots for cutters and shooters.

title: Jokic Passing and Creation
notes: Curated source target for Jokic playmaking context. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Jokic passing and creation make Denver's offense unusual because he can function as a primary playmaker from center. His court vision turns post touches, handoffs, short rolls, and elbow actions into assists, layups, corner threes, and efficient team possessions.

title: Stephen Curry Shot Selection
notes: Curated source target for Curry shot mix and spacing context. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Stephen Curry's three-point volume is historically unusual because his range, quick release, off-ball movement, and screening actions let him create efficient shots at a volume that would be poor shot selection for most players. Analysts describe his shot selection as sustainable because his gravity bends defenses before and after he touches the ball.

title: Victor Wembanyama Two-Way Potential
notes: Exact public Thinking Basketball article on rim pressure and perimeter-player decline; includes original repo-authored Wembanyama summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Victor Wembanyama's rookie profile drew historical center comparisons because of his elite blocks per game, length, rim protection, ball skills, shooting flashes, and offensive versatility. Analysts frame his potential as a two-way center who can deter shots at the rim while stretching and handling on offense.

title: Jayson Tatum Playoff Shot Creation
notes: Exact public Thinking Basketball article on lineup/net-rating sample stability; includes original repo-authored Tatum playoff summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Jayson Tatum playoff analysis often compares regular-season production to postseason efficiency and clutch shot selection. Analysts watch whether his pull-up jumpers, rim pressure, passing reads, and late-game decisions hold up when defenses switch more, load up, and force tougher half-court possessions.

title: Anthony Davis Availability and Impact
notes: Curated source target for Davis availability and impact context. Includes original repo-authored retrieval summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: Anthony Davis availability questions focus on games played, injuries, and whether his team can rely on him across a full season. When healthy, analysts view Davis as an elite two-way player because his rim protection, switch defense, rebounding, lob finishing, and interior scoring can change both shot quality and defensive identity.

title: NBA Stats Glossary
notes: NBA.com robots check timed out during live pass; retained as metadata with original repo-authored glossary summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: NBA Stats glossary concepts include pace, offensive rating, defensive rating, net rating, usage, true shooting, assist percentage, rebound percentage, and effective field goal percentage. These terms normalize performance by possessions, minutes, or opportunities so teams and players can be compared fairly.

title: NBA Stats Play Type Context
notes: NBA.com robots check timed out during live pass; retained as metadata with original repo-authored play-type summary.
full_text: Repo-authored summary: NBA play-type tables classify possessions into actions such as pick-and-roll ball handler, roll man, isolation, post-up, spot-up, handoff, cut, off-screen, putback, and transition. Play-type data helps evaluate offensive identity and efficiency by action rather than by player box score alone.
